hypothesis: Incorporating stereochemical and conformational information through 3D-aware molecular representations—such as encoding torsion angles, chirality descriptors, or conformer-derived fingerprints as an additional textual modality alongside the existing seven representations—will further improve cross-representation alignment in the shared latent space and yield statistically significant performance gains specifically on stereochemistry-sensitive property prediction tasks (e.g., chiral drug bioactivity, enantioselective reaction outcomes) where the current STR-Bamba426M model, trained exclusively on 2D topological notations, is hypothesized to exhibit systematic performance degradation relative to graph-based 3D models such as Uni-Mol.
